label: Combat OS — Foundation / Corrective (Phase 1, 4 weeks)
description: A 4-week spine-friendly foundation block for a lean, advanced striker returning to full training after chiropractic spinal adjustments (cleared to train). Goal: rebuild the muscles striking neglects — glutes/pelvic stability, deep anti-movement core, rotator cuff, mid/lower traps, single-leg stability — and add controlled hypertrophy there, WHILE maintaining strength. Loads capped at RPE 7 on joint-friendly machine and single-leg lifts; no heavy axial barbell work (no back squat, no bent-over row, no deadlift). Run alongside heavy sport practice (boxing, kickboxing, BJJ, classes) and yoga. If sessions feel short, that's by design — bank the extra time in the recovery studio. Graduate to combatos-operator-2026 (Phase 2) when the foundation is rebuilt.

days.label: Day 1 — Legs, Glutes & Deep Core
days.focus: Leg strength without spinal load, glute/pelvic stability, deep core

exercises.name: 45° Leg Press
exercises.cue: Heavy but controlled, full range, mid-foot drive. Loads your legs hard with zero spinal compression — this is how you keep leg strength while the back settles.

exercises.name: Single-Leg Glute Machine (kickback)
exercises.cue: Your preferred machine — elbows supported, full extension, hard squeeze and a beat's pause. Strong glutes = a stable pelvis and a protected lower back. This is the centrepiece of the corrective month.

exercises.name: Single-Leg RDL (DB, light)
exercises.cue: Light — this is hip control and balance, not load. Teaches the clean hinge your spine wants and fires the glute-med that stabilises the hip. Slow and honest.

exercises.name: Dead Bug
exercises.cue: Slow, ribs down, low back glued to the floor — no arching. Deep-core anti-extension is priority #1 post-adjustment: this is what holds your spine together under everything else you do.

days.label: Day 2 — Fight
days.focus: Boxing / kickboxing / BJJ / class — log freely in notes

days.label: Day 3 — Push, Pull & Shoulder Health
days.focus: Spine-friendly pulling, maintained pressing, rotator-cuff & trap rebuild

exercises.name: Chest-Supported Row (plate-loaded)
exercises.cue: Chest on the pad — zero lower-back demand, unlike a bent-over row. Squeeze the shoulder blades, control the return. Rebuilds the mid-back that pulls your posture upright.

exercises.name: Machine Shoulder Press
exercises.cue: Machine keeps it stable this month (swap to barbell OHP if it feels great). Maintains your pressing strength without the axial load of a standing grind.

exercises.name: Cable Face Pull
exercises.cue: The area you flagged as weak. High elbows, pull to the eyes, pause at the back. Rebuilds the postural chain striking neglects — do it religiously this month.

exercises.name: Cable External Rotation
exercises.cue: Light, elbow pinned to your side, slow. Direct rotator-cuff health — the untrained stabiliser that keeps a striker's shoulders in the game for years.

days.label: Day 4 — Fight
days.focus: Boxing / kickboxing / BJJ / class — log freely in notes

days.label: Day 5 — Single-Leg, Vertical Pull & Carries
days.focus: Single-leg strength & symmetry, stable vertical pull, anti-rotation & carry core

exercises.name: Reverse Lunge (DB, controlled)
exercises.cue: Controlled and balanced, drive through the front heel. Single-leg strength and left/right symmetry with minimal spinal load.

exercises.name: Lat Pulldown (machine)
exercises.cue: Machine-stable vertical pulling — keeps your strong lats strong while sparing the grip and joint strain of heavy weighted pull-ups this month.

exercises.name: Pallof Press (cable)
exercises.cue: Resist the rotation, don't create it. Anti-rotation trunk control protects the spine and builds the core that transfers straight into punch power.

exercises.name: Farmer Carry (DB)
exercises.cue: Heavy DBs, stand tall and braced, walk slow and controlled. Loaded carries are the most spine-friendly core builder there is — grip, traps and total-body stability in one. Use the turf track.

days.label: Day 6 — Fight
days.focus: Boxing / kickboxing / BJJ / class — log freely in notes

days.label: Day 7 — Pick / Recovery
days.focus: Yoga or mobility class (great this month), a recovery-studio session (cryo / infrared sauna / plunge), or a bonus session. Log what you did.
